channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Claude goes to college and wants to be your study buddy
item.description: Anthropic’s new Claude for education offers an AI tool for students to support learning over replacing homework.
item.dc:creator: erichs211@gmail.com (Eric Hal Schwartz), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: At 50, Microsoft highlights AI and Copilot as the company’s future
item.description: At a special 50th anniversary event on Friday, Microsoft executives reflected on the company’s storied past and on how it’s now reinventing itself for an AI-focused future.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump holds off on TikTok ban after he almost closed deal with ByteDance, AP source says
item.description: But Beijing hit the brakes on a deal Thursday after Trump announced wide-ranging tariffs around the globe, including against China.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Tech hiring dropped by 29K in March, even as US job growth remained solid
item.description: The tech market lost 29,000 jobs in March, even as the unemployment rate for tech-related jobs slipped 0.1% to 3.1%, according to an analysis of US Labor Department data released today.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Motorola’s new cheap phone offers great value for money – plus it feels like a canvas tote bag
item.description: Motorola has revealed the Edge 60 Fusion handset, which packs solid hardware into a colorful frame – with an option for a unique canvas-style finish.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Windows 11 is getting a very handy change to the taskbar, as Microsoft takes a leaf from Apple’s Mac playbook
item.description: Microsoft could be gunning for big changes to the Windows 11 desktop, as a nifty taskbar tweak emerges following rumored Start menu revamp.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft could make a huge change to an iconic part of Windows 11 that you use every day, and I can’t wait to try the new Start menu
item.description: A redesign for the Windows 11 Start menu has been spotted in testing, and it comes with the change I’ve been desperate to see.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Businesses are losing millions to fraud every year
item.description: Large businesses could be losing in excess of $50 million a year to identity fraud – stronger systems are required.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Why Multicloud Adoption is Failing in 2025
item.description: The rapid adoption of AI workloads has overwhelmed multicloud management strategies, pushing enterprises to the brink of chaos.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google’s Gemini 2.5 Pro could be the most important AI model so far this year
item.description: Google released its new Gemini 2.5 Pro Experimental AI model late last month, and it’s quickly stacked up top marks on a number of coding, math, and reasoning benchmark tests—making it a contender for the world’s best model right now.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Sonatype warns of 18,000 open source malware packages
item.description: Software supply chain security company Sonatype uncovered 17,954 open-source malware packages during Q1 2025, the company revealed in its Open Source Malware Index. Sonatype’s Open Source Malware Index for Q1 2025 was introduced April 2.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Leadership, trust, and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare
item.description: From ensuring the quality of data to fostering a culture of experimentation and adaptability, leaders must actively steer AI’s integration into operations. The post Leadership, trust, and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Visa unveils a trio of new tools to make the payments process easier
item.description: At Visa’s ETA Transact event on April 3, the payments giant introduced three new products designed to simplify and secure payment acceptance. These innovations—Authorize.
item.dc:creator: Lily McDonald, Fast Company

item.title: GitHub upgrades tooling to help developers stop leaking secrets
item.description: Over 39 million API keys, credentials, and other secrets leaked onto GitHub’s platform last year, but an update to its scanning tool could help stop that.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok deal will be reached before deadline: Vance
item.description: Vice President Vance said a TikTok deal would be reached before Saturday's ban enforcement deadline after President Trump’s closed-door meeting with top aides to discuss potential investors. “It’ll come out before the deadline,” Vance said in a Thursday morning interview with Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Musk will remain 'a friend and an adviser' to Trump after leaving DOGE: Vance
item.description: Vice President Vance said Thursday that billionaire Elon Musk will remain a “friend and an adviser” to the White House after Musk departs from his role as a special government employee next month.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Detroit production of Mozart opera turns its female characters into automatons
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Audiences at the Detroit Opera House expecting their performance of “Cosi fan tutte" to begin with the overture may be surprised to hear instead a product launch from a tech company CEO.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SILVERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dow wants to power its Texas manufacturing complex with new nuclear reactors instead of natural gas
item.description: Dow, a major producer of chemicals and plastics, wants to use next-generation nuclear reactors for clean power and steam at a Texas manufacturing complex instead of natural gas. Dow's subsidiary, Long Mott Energy, applied Monday to the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission for a construction permit.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New phishing attack uses real-time interception to bypass 2FA
item.description: This phishing kit bypasses 2FA via session hijacking and real-time credential theft. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers four ways to stay safe from Astaroth phishing attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DoD Modernization Exchange 2025: Savan Kong on building first Pentagon CX Office from the ground up
item.description: DoD’s former CXO shares details from the young office’s efforts to begin scaling customer experience across the department. The post DoD Modernization Exchange 2025: Savan Kong on building first Pentagon CX Office from the ground up first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: AI enables paralyzed man to control robotic arm with brain signals
item.description: The combination of artificial intelligence and neuroscience allows a paralyzed man to manipulate a robotic arm by using his brain to imagine movements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
